questions.question: 1 (a) Compound X is a solid that contains a cobalt cation and one type of anion. A small amount of X is added to deionised water and the mixture stirred until the solid dissolves. Tests are carried out on separate samples of the solution of X. Complete the table. TestObservationInference (i)Note the appearance A pink solutionThe formula of the complex ion formed is ..................
questions.text: 1 (a) Compound X is a solid that contains a cobalt cation and one type of anion. A small amount of X is added to deionised water and the mixture stirred until the solid dissolves. Tests are carried out on separate samples of the solution of X. Complete the table. TestObservationInference (i)Note the appearance A pink solutionThe formula of the complex ion formed is ..................

parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following points: • known mass of CoCl ∙xH O(s) measured • heat sample (strongly) in crucible / evaporating basin (with lid using blue flame on Bunsen burner) • until mass of sample is constant (to ensure all water is removed / all CoCl ∙xH O(s) has broken down) • difference in (initial and final) mass (= mass of water removed) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow weight for mass Allow a clearly annotated diagram Do not award use of beaker/ conical flask/test tube Do not award use of heating mantle Ignore heat until all the water is removed/evaporated Allow M3 and M4 for method involving a desiccator/oven but not for the addition of drying agent Do not award M4 for response involving recrystallisation or distillation Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark • filter to remove sand / to separate the sodium hydroxide • rinse (sand) with deionised / distilled water (to ensure all the sodium hydroxide solution is separated) (1) (1) M1 can be shown with a suitable labelled diagram Accept decant or description of Allow solid for sand Allow use of Buchner funnel Allow use of sieve Ignore hot/cold references to filtration Allow rinse with solvent Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark • use of (250 cm ) volumetric flask • add deionised / distilled water and up to the mark and mix (1) (1) Allow graduated / standard flask Do not allow beaker/conical flask/measuring cylinder M2 independent of M1 but reference to ‘mark’ or volume line or 250 cm required Allow any indication of mixing e.g. shake, invert, swirl Allow ‘pure water’ Just ‘water’ is insufficient unless qualified at some point Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark

• (M1) calculation of initial moles of NaOH AND calculation of moles of HCl • (M2) calculation of moles of NaOH in 250 cm • (M3) calculation of moles of NaOH that reacted with (NH ) SO • (M4) calculation of mass of (NH ) SO • (M5) calculation of percentage by mass of (NH ) SO in fertiliser (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation (20 ÷ 1000) × 1 = 0.02 (mol) (12.75 ÷ 1000) × 0.0500 = 6.375 × 10 – (mol) 6.375 × 10 – × (250 ÷ 25) = 6.375 × 10 – (mol) 0.02 − 6.375 × 10 – = 0.013625 (mol) 0.013625 ÷ 2 × 132.1 = 0.89993 (g) Allow TE from M1 to M4 (0.89993 ÷ 5.75) × 100 = 15.651 % = 15.7 % Allow TE from M4 to M5 if answer is < 100 % Ignore SF Note penalise rounding errors once only in M1-M5 Final answer without working scores (5) 22.2 % scores (4) omission of x10 in step 2 31.3 % scores (4) omission of ÷ 2 in step 4 44.5% scores (3) omission of x10 and ÷ 2 62.6% scores (4) multiplied by 2 instead of ÷ 2 Allow use of 132 for step 4 Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark • NH (remaining) will react with the HCl / acid (in the titration) • so titre would be greater • so (it would appear that there is a) lower percentage of (NH ) SO (in the fertiliser) (1) (1) (1) Allow reference to neutralisation M2 dependent on some attempt at M1 such as ammonia remaining Allow less (NH ) SO (in the fertiliser) M3 can be awarded provided there is reference to greater titre Allow (1) as a TE if titre is stated to be smaller which means the percentage will be higher Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark • place (damp) red litmus (paper into vapour) and which should remain red (if all NH removed) Do not award dip the litmus into the solution Allow (litmus) goes blue if any NH remains Ignore reference to pungent smell Allow place (damp) universal indicator (paper) into vapour and which should remain yellow (if all NH removed) / goes blue or purple if any NH remains Allow place stopper of conc. HCl bottle near mouth of reaction vessel/ use HCl(g) and which will not for

• calculation of mass of ethanol and calculation of mass of butanoic acid • calculation of moles of ethanol and calculation of moles of butanoic acid and statement that moles of butanoic acid > moles of ethanol / butanoic acid is in excess (because the ratio is 1:1) (1) (1) Example of calculation 5 × 0.79 = 3.95 (g) 10 × 0.96 = 9.6 (g) 3.95 ÷ 46 = 0.085870 (mol) 9.6 ÷ 88 = 0.1091 (mol) 0.1091 > 0.085870 (mol) Allow statement that moles of ethanol < moles of butanoic acid Allow TE from M1 to M2 Ignore SF Allow (2) for n(ethanol) = 0.085870 and n(butanoic acid) = 0.1091 so butanoic acid in excess because the masses must have been determined Calculation dividing molar mass by density scores (0) Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark • CO is produced which increases pressure / to prevent build up of pressure from the CO produced (inside funnel if tap not opened / stopper not removed) • H SO + 2NaHCO → Na SO + 2H O + 2CO / C H COOH + NaHCO → C H COO (−) Na (+) + H O + CO / H + + HCO – → CO + H O H + + NaHCO → Na + + CO + H O (1) (1) Allow just ‘pressure increases/to relieve pressure’ provided CO given as a product in an equation even if the equation is incorrect Allow ‘gas is produced which increases pressure’ Do not allow if incorrect gas stated Do not award reference to explosion Ignore state symbols even if incorrect Accept other formulae except molecular Allow H SO + NaHCO →NaHSO + H O+CO Use of RCOOH in equation Number Acceptable Answers Additional Guidance Mark Any two from three Either • aqueous layer will not flow (from the funnel when tap is open) • as stopper prevents air from entering top of funnel (to keep pressure constant) • due to creation of lower pressure / vacuum (inside) Or • some of the (aqueous) liquid flows out (of the funnel) • bubbles of air (enter through tap) / flow is stopped • (which) mixes/disturbs the two layers (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Do not award for liquid flow due to ‘normal’ functioning of separating funnel Number Acceptable
